profiles.name: Analytical Scientist
profiles.description: Approaches problems through structured, evidence-first reasoning and is comfortable with the technical vocabulary of research.

profiles.name: Evidence-Oriented Thinker
profiles.description: Combines strong evidence literacy with genuine openness to revising conclusions as new evidence appears — a core EBM disposition.

profiles.name: Methodical Researcher
profiles.description: Pairs rigorous reasoning with strong follow-through; likely to thrive in structured research or data-heavy coursework.

profiles.name: Curious Skeptic
profiles.description: Strong instinct to question and analyze, but evidence-specific vocabulary/skills are still developing — a very common and easily-addressed early-stage pattern.

profiles.name: Structured Innovator
profiles.description: Brings organized, analytical thinking to new problems, with a preference for having a clear plan before diving in.

profiles.name: Data-Driven Communicator
profiles.description: A relatively rare, high-value combination: technically rigorous *and* able to translate that rigor for a non-technical audience.

profiles.name: Independent-Minded Reasoner
profiles.description: Reasons confidently and is comfortable reaching conclusions independently; benefits from deliberately seeking outside checks on those conclusions (see IH-01 interaction, Part 3 §4.3, if it also fires).

profiles.name: Compassionate Communicator
profiles.description: Consistently prioritizes the human side of a situation — listening, honesty, and patient-centeredness — across both communication and ethics scenarios.

profiles.name: Adaptive Clinician
profiles.description: Comfortable with uncertainty, open to correction, and attentive to others — a combination well-matched to the ambiguity of real clinical encounters.

profiles.name: Reflective Practitioner
profiles.description: Pairs genuine openness to being wrong with strong ethical-reasoning tendencies — likely to seek and use feedback constructively.

profiles.name: Empathic Realist
profiles.description: Comfortable sitting with both emotional complexity and factual uncertainty at the same time, without needing to resolve either prematurely.

profiles.name: Patient Advocate
profiles.description: A three-domain combination emphasizing person-centered, ethically grounded comfort with uncertainty — thematically aligned with shared decision-making.

profiles.name: Principled Skeptic
profiles.description: Reasons rigorously, holds firm ethical standards, and remains open to being shown wrong — a combination that guards against both under- and over-confidence.

profiles.name: Rigorous Humanist
profiles.description: A deliberately-named counter-profile to the EC-01 interaction pattern (Part 3 §4.3) — this profile requires *both* SAR and ECPC High simultaneously, i.e., it can only match respondents who do **not** trigger EC-01, since EC-01 requires ECPC=Low.

profiles.name: Calm Communicator
profiles.description: Stays composed and attentive to others even under stress — a combination especially relevant to difficult-conversation and bad-news scenarios.

profiles.name: Resilient Learner
profiles.description: Combines strong follow-through with strong recovery from setbacks — well-positioned for the sustained demands of training.

profiles.name: Steady Under Pressure
profiles.description: Tolerates both emotional strain and informational uncertainty without becoming destabilized by either.

profiles.name: Growth-Minded Trainee
profiles.description: Recovers well from setbacks *and* treats them as genuine learning opportunities rather than just something to get through.

profiles.name: Steady Ethical Anchor
profiles.description: A three-domain combination suggesting consistency under pressure — worth noting the ERP domain's wider confidence margin (Part 1 §2.6) applies here as it does everywhere ERP contributes.

profiles.name: Detail-Oriented Planner
profiles.description: Thrives with structure and clear plans; may find genuinely unclear situations more effortful than most, which is a workable growth edge, not a weakness.

profiles.name: Balanced Generalist
profiles.description: No domain scored clearly high or low — a genuinely common and entirely valid pattern reflecting broad, even development across all eight domains rather than a standout specialty.

profiles.name: Emerging Clinician-in-Training
profiles.description: Early-stage profile for respondents who show general analytical/organizational engagement but haven't yet built EBM-specific vocabulary — an expected, addressable starting point, not a readiness concern.

profiles.name: High-Achieving Planner
profiles.description: Distinct from `PROF-20`: this profile keys directly off the raw CSR score (>=90) rather than the tier system, since the perfectionism-relevant pattern is specifically about the *very* top of the range, not simply 'High.'

profiles.name: Sustainable Achiever
profiles.description: The constructive counter-pattern to the CR-02 'overextension risk' flag (Part 3 §4.3) — strong follow-through paired with strong (not low) resilience and openness, suggesting sustainable rather than strained high performance.
